text:
Market today: Cautiously risk-on. Fed independence anxiety eased after the Supreme Court blocked Trump from firing Lisa Cook — that's a small positive for rate-sensitive tech. Oil stabilizing near $70 on the Iran deal reduces one inflation wildcard, but it's not a catalyst for your chip/tech names directly.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 15% and it's your largest position; on margin that pain is amplified, and it's one bad day from testing your $336 stop. PLTR is down nearly 10% with a SELL flag — no reason to hold a loser that's flagging exit. PTNM is down 15% in a tiny spec with thin data — easiest cut on the board. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; treat it like it can move 20% either way on any given day.

Watch: (1) Whether AVGO holds above $336 — a break triggers your stop on your largest, margin-amplified position. (2) Any AI infrastructure news; severe weather disrupting data centers could rattle the whole sector.

Bottom line: Trim or exit your flagged losers before margin forces your hand.
